prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for ferrocene (21 atoms: Fe + 2×Cp rings) were computed at the GFN2-xTB level (xtb backend) in the gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. The N±1 states used doublet multiplicity (auto-derived). Partial charges use the Mulliken scheme (GFN2-xTB).

Headline results: The iron centre (atom 11) is both the most electrophilic site (f⁺ = 0.22885456) and the most nucleophilic site (f⁻ = 0.25938191; reported as the headline value). The dual descriptor for Fe is −0.030527, confirming it as a net nucleophilic/donating centre. All C and H atoms on the Cp rings carry comparatively small Fukui values (~0.012–0.064) and positive dual descriptors.

Integrity: status=ok, trustworthy=true. sum(f⁺)=1.0000, sum(f⁻)=1.0000. No warnings reported by the engine.

Method caveat: GFN2-xTB is a semi-empirical tight-binding Hamiltonian; Mulliken-based Fukui indices are indicative of reactivity trends but are not chemically-accurate absolute values.
